edges.description: Abnormally organized collagen prevents proper mineral deposition, resulting in severe skeletal hypomineralization.
Multiple rib fractures and thoracic hypomineralization restrict thoracic volume, mechanically limiting fetal lung growth.

nodes.description: Ribs are severely abnormal in OI type II, most characteristically showing continuous beading from repeated fractures (type IIA) or thin beading (type IIC). All subtypes show marked rib shortening and broadening, producing a narrow thoracic cavity.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000772 | Abnormal rib morphology | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: Osteogenesis imperfecta type II delineation of the phenotype with reference to genetic heterogeneity.
evidence.snippet: bones, angulation of tibiae and continuously beaded ribs.

nodes.description: The thorax is small and bell-shaped, reflecting severe thoracic restriction.

evidence.reference_title: Cyclic intravenous pamidronate for an infant with osteogenesis imperfecta type II.
evidence.snippet: Fetal CT at 28 weeks' gestation showed decreased ossification of the skull, a small bell-shaped thorax, hypoplastic vertebrae, and shortening and bowing of the long bones, leading to the diagnosis of osteogenesis imperfecta (OI) type II.

nodes.description: Blue sclerae are part of the external connective-tissue phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Cardiovascular pathology in osteogenesis imperfecta type IIA with a review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Lethal perinatal osteogenesis imperfecta (OI Type II) is a biochemically diverse collagen disorder characterized by short, crumpled long bones, beaded ribs, blue sclerae and thin, fragile skin.

nodes.description: Long bones are severely deformed and crumpled from repeated prenatal fractures.

evidence.reference_title: A case of chondrodysplasia punctata with features of osteogenesis imperfecta type II.
evidence.snippet: All groups have long bones described as "wrinkled" or "crumpled" secondary to repeated fractures.

nodes.description: The calvarium is poorly ossified on fetal imaging.

nodes.description: The calvarium shows markedly reduced ossification on imaging, contributing to the soft calvarium palpable at birth in type II OI.

nodes.description: The fetal skeleton is diffusely hypomineralized, with reduced bone density on prenatal imaging.

evidence.reference_title: Prenatal diagnosis of osteogenesis imperfecta type II.
evidence.snippet: All of subtype A exhibited typical triad of bone shortening, diffuse hypomineralization and multiple fractures of long bones including beaded ribs whereas the subtype B showed shortening of only femurs, normal bone echodensity and isolated fractures of long bones.

nodes.description: Glycine substitutions in the Gly-X-Y repeat of the collagen triple helical domain disrupt normal helix formation. The abnormal alpha chains are incorporated into trimers, producing structurally defective collagen that cannot form proper fibrils (dominant-negative effect). This results in severe deficiency of functional bone matrix.

evidence.reference_title: Perinatal lethal osteogenesis imperfecta.
evidence.snippet: The mutations act in a dominant negative manner as the mutant pro alpha chains are incorporated into type I procollagen molecules that also contain normal pro alpha chains. The abnormal molecules are poorly secreted, more susceptible to degradation, and impair the formation of the extracellular matrix.

nodes.description: Structurally abnormal collagen prevents proper mineral deposition in bone matrix. The disorganized collagen fibrils cannot support normal hydroxyapatite crystal nucleation and growth, resulting in severe skeletal hypomineralization — diffusely decreased bone density, multiple prenatal fractures, and crumpled long bones with beaded ribs. This skeletal fragility underlies the lethal phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Perinatal lethal osteogenesis imperfecta.
evidence.snippet: The collagen fibres are abnormally organised and mineralisation is impaired.

nodes.description: Severely affected fetuses with OI type II may show intrauterine growth retardation in addition to the skeletal abnormalities.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001511 | Intrauterine growth retardation | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: Prenatal diagnosis of osteogenesis imperfecta type II.
evidence.snippet: polyhydramnios, oligohydramnios, hydrop fetalis and small for gestational age.

nodes.description: Numerous fractures are already present in utero or at birth and involve the long bones and ribs.

evidence.reference_title: Prenatal diagnosis of osteogenesis imperfecta type II.
evidence.snippet: All of subtype A exhibited typical triad of bone shortening, diffuse hypomineralization and multiple fractures of long bones including beaded ribs whereas the subtype B showed shortening of only femurs, normal bone echodensity and isolated fractures of long bones.

nodes.description: Repeated rib fractures produce the characteristic beaded rib appearance on imaging.

evidence.reference_title: A case of chondrodysplasia punctata with features of osteogenesis imperfecta type II.
evidence.snippet: In groups A and C, the chest is generally small, with thickened and shortened ribs, and each rib has characteristic "beading" patterns secondary to repeated fracturing.

nodes.description: Severe respiratory failure at birth results from pulmonary hypoplasia and thoracic restriction. This is the terminal event in the vast majority of OI type II cases. Rarely, aggressive neonatal respiratory support allows short-term survival.

evidence.reference_title: Perinatal lethal type II osteogenesis imperfecta: a case report.
evidence.snippet: Death occurred on day 25 of life related to respiratory failure.

nodes.description: Lung development is markedly reduced and contributes to perinatal lethality.

evidence.reference_title: Pulmonary hypoplasia and osteogenesis imperfecta type II with defective synthesis of alpha I(1) procollagen.
evidence.snippet: Pulmonary hypoplasia has been previously observed in OI type II, but has not been defined clinically. The infant described herein was born with OI type II and pulmonary hypoplasia.

nodes.description: Severe respiratory compromise is present at birth and drives early mortality.

evidence.reference_title: Perinatal lethal type II osteogenesis imperfecta: a case report.
evidence.snippet: At birth, the newborn presented immediate respiratory distress. Postnatal examination and bone radiography confirmed the diagnosis of OI type IIA. Death occurred on day 25 of life related to respiratory failure.

nodes.description: Marked shortening of the limbs is a defining prenatal manifestation.

evidence.snippet: A group of fetuses with a perinatally lethal variety of osteogenesis imperfecta (O.I. type II) is characterized by short limbs, and clinical and roentgenological evidence of severe osseous fragility and defective ossification.

evidence.snippet: Lethal perinatal osteogenesis imperfecta (OI Type II) is a biochemically diverse collagen disorder characterized by short, crumpled long bones, beaded ribs, blue sclerae and thin, fragile skin.

nodes.description: Multiple rib fractures from severe bone fragility produce a small, bell-shaped thorax that mechanically restricts fetal lung growth. Defective collagen in pulmonary connective tissue may also directly impair alveolar development. The resulting pulmonary hypoplasia causes respiratory insufficiency at birth and is the proximate cause of perinatal lethality in OI type II.

evidence.reference_title: Pulmonary hypoplasia and osteogenesis imperfecta type II with defective synthesis of alpha I(1) procollagen.
evidence.snippet: These findings suggest that biochemically regulated processes, as well as mechanical factors, may impeded pulmonary development in similar cases of OI type II.

nodes.description: Hypoplastic vertebrae are seen on fetal imaging in OI type II, reflecting the severe impairment of ossification that affects the axial skeleton.

evidence.reference_title: Cyclic intravenous pamidronate for an infant with osteogenesis imperfecta type II.
